verses.text: He commends Phebe, 'a servant of the church,' to be received in the Lord. A faithful sister. He honours the women who serve.
He commends Phebe, 'a servant of the church,' to be received in the Lord. A faithful sister. He honours the women who serve.
Greet Priscilla and Aquila, who 'laid down their own necks' for him, and the church in their house. Brave friends. They risked their lives for the gospel.
Greet Priscilla and Aquila, who 'laid down their own necks' for him, and the church in their house. Brave friends. They risked their lives for the gospel.
Greet Priscilla and Aquila, who 'laid down their own necks' for him, and the church in their house. Brave friends. They risked their lives for the gospel.
Greetings to Mary, Andronicus, and his many fellow-labourers in Christ. Honoured workers. Paul remembers each by name.
Greetings to Mary, Andronicus, and his many fellow-labourers in Christ. Honoured workers. Paul remembers each by name.
Greetings to Mary, Andronicus, and his many fellow-labourers in Christ. Honoured workers. Paul remembers each by name.
Greetings to Mary, Andronicus, and his many fellow-labourers in Christ. Honoured workers. Paul remembers each by name.
Salute Apelles, the household of Narcissus, and the women 'who laboured in the Lord.' Diligent saints. Their toil for Christ is not forgotten.
Salute Apelles, the household of Narcissus, and the women 'who laboured in the Lord.' Diligent saints. Their toil for Christ is not forgotten.
Salute Apelles, the household of Narcissus, and the women 'who laboured in the Lord.' Diligent saints. Their toil for Christ is not forgotten.
Salute Rufus and the brethren; 'greet one another with an holy kiss.' Warm fellowship. The churches are bound in mutual love.
Salute Rufus and the brethren; 'greet one another with an holy kiss.' Warm fellowship. The churches are bound in mutual love.
Salute Rufus and the brethren; 'greet one another with an holy kiss.' Warm fellowship. The churches are bound in mutual love.
Salute Rufus and the brethren; 'greet one another with an holy kiss.' Warm fellowship. The churches are bound in mutual love.
Mark them that 'cause divisions'; by 'good words and fair speeches' they deceive the simple. Beware. Smooth talk can hide a divisive heart.
Mark them that 'cause divisions'; by 'good words and fair speeches' they deceive the simple. Beware. Smooth talk can hide a divisive heart.
Be 'wise unto that which is good'; 'the God of peace shall bruise Satan under your feet shortly.' Sure victory. The promised crushing of Satan is near.
Be 'wise unto that which is good'; 'the God of peace shall bruise Satan under your feet shortly.' Sure victory. The promised crushing of Satan is near.
Timothy and others send greeting; Tertius, who wrote the epistle, salutes them. Faithful company. The letter comes with many loving names.
Timothy and others send greeting; Tertius, who wrote the epistle, salutes them. Faithful company. The letter comes with many loving names.
Timothy and others send greeting; Tertius, who wrote the epistle, salutes them. Faithful company. The letter comes with many loving names.
'To God only wise, be glory through Jesus Christ for ever'; the gospel is made known to all nations. Doxology. The book ends in praise to the all-wise God.
